Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL's history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: 'Have not left any party': MLC Sachin Ahir insists after Shinde Sena entry; no whip applies, say officials
Article Text:
MLC Sachin Ahir has filed his nomination for the deputy chairperson of the council, a move that has weakened Shiv Sena (UBT) to just five members.

MLC Says Will Explain Legal & Technical Aspects Later

Team UBT may lose claim to LoP’s post

Ahir may be formidable rival against Aaditya in Worli in ’29





MUMBAI: MLC Sachin Ahir filed nomination for the post of deputy chairperson of the council, and Mahayuti, with its numbers, is expected to easily win the election which will be held on Wednesday.With Ahir leaving, Sena (UBT) is left with just 5 members in the council. Legislature officials said the nomination for the deputy chairperson's poll is not filed through any party, so it would not attract the anti-defection law. Officials also said Ahir was elected as MLC of undivided Shiv Sena in 2022, so he could not be disqualified now. Sena (UBT) MP Arvind Sawant called Ahir's move a violation of Schedule 10.However, Ahir said despite joining Shinde Sena, "I have not left any party. I will explain the legal and technical aspects later." Shinde too dismissed concerns over disqualification, saying Ahir was originally elected from the undivided Shiv Sena. "When Ahir became MLC, all of us voted for him. He contested on Shiv Sena's bow-and-arrow symbol, which is now with us. So there is nothing to worry about," Shinde said. A legislature official said, "Since Ahir is contesting for deputy chairperson, no party whip will apply to him."Shinde said last week six (UBT Sena) MPs had come to the Sena "and hit sixes, and now Ahir has hit sixes". Ahir filed his nomination in the presence of CM Devendra Fadnavis and Dy CMs Shinde and Sunetra Pawar.Aaditya Thackeray said this is not 'Operation Tiger' but 'Operation Devendra Fadnavis'. He added, "Ahir cannot even say Aaditya did not meet him. He is an MLC, his daughter was made ward committee member. He was also given the post of deputy leader. So what more do you want? Should we now perform your 'aarti' every day? Shiv Sena (UBT) does not care if someone leaves."Ahir said he would respond at the right time to the barbs hurled at him from Sena (UBT). "As long as we were together, I was good. Some people are now trying to tell me how worthless I am."With MLC Sachin Ahir’s exit, the Shiv Sena (UBT) is likely to lose its claim over the leader of opposition’s (LoP) post in the legislative council. The Sena (UBT) is left with only 5 MLCs now, and the Congress party too has 5 MLCs in the 78-member council.Political observers said it is unlikely that the council will get an LoP in the near future. The post in the council has been vacant since August 2025, when Shiv Sena (UBT)’s MLC Ambadas Danve retired. Danve has now been reelected to the council, but a decision on the LoP hasn’t been taken yet. The state is now without an LoP in both houses of the legislature. This has happened for the first time in several years, senior officials said.An LoP in the assembly hasn’t been appointed by Speaker Rahul Narwekar since Dec 2024, despite Sena (UBT) giving its MLA Bhaskar Jadhav’s name, citing technical reasons as Sena (UBT) doesn’t have MLAs comprising 10% of total strength of the assembly. Narwekar has maintained that rules are being studied to see if LoP’s post can be given if a party has less than 10% MLAs.Sachin Ahir’s exit from Shiv Sena (UBT) has come as a setback for Aaditya Thackeray as Ahir could be a formidable candidate from the Worli assembly constituency and a potential political threat for Aaditya, who is MLA from that seat.Political observers said the Dy CM Eknath Shinde-led Sena would prepare the ground in Worli for the 2029 assembly polls and pitch Ahir against Aaditya. Ahir, a former MLA from Worli, is known to have a grassroots network there, and with the backing of Shinde Sena and BJP, he could pose a challenge to Aaditya. In the 2024 assembly polls, Aaditya managed to win from the Worli constituency by a margin of just 8,801 votes.This was a massive decrease in margin from the 2019 polls, when he had won by 67,427 votes. Aaditya defeated Sena’s Milind Deora in 2024. “In 2024, Deora was fielded at the last minute and he was not a local. Ahir knows the constituency well and is a Worli local. He also has clout in the mill workers’ and other unions in central Mumbai, so that will also work to his advantage,” a political observer said.In 2019, Ahir had joined Shiv Sena on the eve of the assembly elections. Aaditya then fought from Worli and eventually Ahir was given an MLC berth. Asked if he will fight the 2029 assembly polls from Worli, Ahir said on Tuesday, ”I will try to fulfil any responsibility Eknath Shinde will give me.”
Article Summary: MLC Sachin Ahir has filed his nomination for the deputy chairperson of the council, a move that has weakened Shiv Sena (UBT) to just five members. Mahayuti, with its numbers, is expected to easily win the election which will be held on Wednesday. Ahir said despite joining Shinde Sena, "I have not left any party. I will explain the legal and technical aspects later"

Article Title: Legendary theatre and film personality Vijaya Mehta passes away at 91
Article Text:
Vijaya Mehta’s riveting, slice-of-life creations include the poignant teleplay ‘Hamidabai Ki Kothi’ and ‘Pestonjee’

Mumbai:





Legendary theatre and film personality Vijaya Mehta, known simply as Bai to generations of theatre lovers and moviegoers, passed away at age 91 Tuesday.Born in Vadodara on November 4, 1934, Vijayabai found her calling as a teenager. She met theatre doyen Ebrahim Alkazi at age 18, whom she went on to consider her guru.Her riveting, slice-of-life creations include the poignant teleplay ‘Hamidabai Ki Kothi’. This narrative delineated the arrival of musical recordings by classical singers, and the consequential decline of live kotha culture. “People could just press a button and listen to songs, they would not visit the courtesans anymore,” she said.Her cerebral -- not caricaturist -- portrayal of the Parsi community in the Shabana Azmi-Naseeruddin Shah-starrer ‘Pestonjee’ drew partly from her second marriage to Farokh Mehta. Vijayabai’s first husband Harin Khote, son of Durga Khote, had passed away early.Her entry to Hindi cinema was facilitated by Govind Nihalani in his award-winning movie ‘Party’, which offered insight into the lifestyles of the rich and famous, and Shyam Benegal’s ‘Kalyug’.The first full length feature film she directed was ‘Rao Saheb’ in 1986 followed by ‘Smriti Chitre’.She was founder-member of the Mumbai-based theatre group Rangayan along with Vijay Tendulkar, Arvind Deshpande and Shriram Lagoo. Rangayan went on to shape the fortunes of experimental theatre.Her commitment to stage plays lasted a lifetime and Vijayabai became executive director of the National Centre for Performing Arts (NCPA) in her later years. Her proactive presence moulded a milestone era in its history.“I was, in fact, involved with NCPA from its conceptualisation. I had attended the inauguration of Tata Theatre. I went on to organise several Indo-German collaborations of Indian classics from here. I organised workshops with international greats including Peter Brooks,” she said, at its 50th anniversary.Vijayabai’s eloquent artistic expression, sharp eye for detail, her keen sense of humour and the ability to draw out the best from stalwart performers like Shabana Azmi, Naseeruddin Shah, Tanvi Azmi, Neena Kulkarni, Nana Patekar, and Bharati Achrekar has been much discussed.The world of Indian theatre and cinema is impoverished by her loss.
Article Summary: Her riveting, slice-of-life creations include the poignant teleplay ‘Hamidabai Ki Kothi’ She met theatre doyen Ebrahim Alkazi at age 18, whom she went on to consider her guru. Vijayabai became executive director of the National Centre for Performing Arts.

Article Title: Row over scrapping of British-era girder from Keshavrao Bhosale Natyagruha and Shahu Khasbaug maidan
Article Text:
Keshavrao Bhosale Natyagruha was gutted in fire on Aug 28, 2024

Kolhapur:





A group of theatre artistes and social activists in Kolhapur have accused the Kolhapur Municipal Corporation (KMC) of unauthorized actions regarding historical artifacts.According to them, during the ongoing renovation works at the Keshavrao Bhosale Natyagruha and Shahu Khasbaug Kusti maidan, the KMC disposed of 25 heritage girders and beams as scrap.These architectural pieces, which feature significant historical markings and inscriptions, were originally imported from Britain by Rajarshi Shahu Maharaj over a century ago. The activists contend that the municipal body lacked the legal authority to sell these items, particularly because they failed to secure the mandatory approval from the Heritage Committee.History researcher Indrajit Sawant questioned the legality of the KMC’s actions. “Rajarshi Shahu Maharaj had specially ordered the girders from London a hundred years ago, and they had important inscriptions on them. How could these girders be sold without the permission of the Heritage Committee?” Sawant asked.The civic action committee alleged that after the theatre caught fire, the scrap was sold for mere Rs 7 lakh, keeping the Heritage committee in the dark.Ashok Powar, from the civic action committee, said, “The shed which KMC has constructed now at the Shahu Khasbaug maidan royal gallery looks more like a crematorium shed. The current structure doesn’t match the heritage tag. Action should be taken against the responsible officials. ”A video of a public representative allegedly making a deal with the company appointed for the reconstruction of Keshavrao Bhosale Natyagruh has been going viral for the past two days. Theatre artist Sunil Ghorpade warned that if any truth was found in it and the quality of the theatre construction deteriorated, a strong protest would be launched.Speaking over this, the KMC-appointed consultant informed that after chemical testing, the girders that got damaged in the fire were found to be unsuitable for reuse. Consultant Chetan Raikar said, “The iron girders, which had been exposed to temperatures of 300 to 400 degrees Celsius in the fire of the fire tragedy that took place on Aug 8, 2024, were unusable. Its capacity was tested and chemically separated at that time. ”Meanwhile, a review meeting was held on Tuesday, chaired by Mayor Rouparani Nikam and KMC Commissioner Dr Rajendra Bharud, over the progress of Keshavrao Bhosale Natyagruha.The chairperson of the heritage committee submitted demands to the mayor regarding specific requirements at the actual site.
Article Summary: The Kolhapur Municipal Corporation (KMC) disposed of 25 heritage girders and beams as scrap. These architectural pieces, which feature significant historical markings and inscriptions, were originally imported from Britain by Rajarshi Shahu Maharaj over a century ago. The activists contend that the municipal body lacked the legal authority to sell these items.

Article Title: Teen and sibling thrashed after spat on train in Navi Mumbai
Article Text:
Another incident of verbal spat between a 16-year-old teenage boy and a 20-year-old youth took place in a harbour line train in Navi Mumbai on June 24 night, after the youth accidentally stamped on the teen's foot. (File photo used for representational purpose)





NAVI MUMBAI: A day after the Borivli train murder incident following a violent altercation between two commuters that took place on June 23, yet another incident of verbal spat between a 16-year-old teenage boy and a 20-year-old youth took place in a harbour line train in Navi Mumbai on June 24 night, after the youth accidentally stamped on the teen's foot.The altercation later spilled on to the street, when the youth accompanied by his two friends waylaid the teen near Seawoods Grand Central Mall around 9. 30pm and assaulted him with kicks and fist blows.As the teen's elder brother intervened to rescue him, the trio assaulted him as well in which he sustained a hand fracture.The injured brothers were identified as Rony Rana Shaikh (22) and Alkas Rana Shaikh (16).While, the accused youth was identified as Rohit Surendra Bhosale (22), all residing in Tata Nagar slum pocket, sector 10, CBD-Belapur near Konkan Bhawan.Accused Rohit Bhosale and his two unidentified aides were booked under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) sections for voluntarily causing grievous hurt and joint criminal liability.
Article Summary: A day after the Borivli train murder incident following a violent altercation between two commuters that took place on June 23, yet another incident of verbal spat took place in a harbour line train in Navi Mumbai on June 24 night. The altercation later spilled on to the street, when the youth accompanied by his two friends waylaid the teen and assaulted him with kicks and fist blows.

Article Title: 4 youngsters trapped in elevator of Navi Mumbai high-rise for 90 minutes; rescued safely by firemen
Article Text: NAVI MUMBAI: Four youngsters were trapped inside an elevator between the third and fourth floors of Tulip CHS in Sector 34B, Kharghar, for about 90 minutes from 3 am on Friday due to a power outage in the residential area.The Kharghar fire brigade received the emergency call at 4.25 am and reached the society at 4.35 am.Firefighters rescued the trapped individuals — two men and two women, all aged between 20 and 22 — by forcing open the elevator door from outside using a crowbar and a battery-operated spreader tool.Prashant Darekar, a leading fireman at Kharghar, said,"It took us less than 5 minutes to rescue the trapped youngsters. While, one woman was a tenant in the building, the other three were her friends who had come to drop her home after they returned from a night out."
Article Summary: Four youngsters were trapped inside an elevator between the third and fourth floors of Tulip CHS in Sector 34B, Kharghar, for about 90 minutes from 3 am on Friday due to a power outage. Firefighters rescued the trapped individuals — two men and two women, all aged between 20 and 22 — by forcing open the elevator door from outside.

Article Title: Tree falls on man in Thane; 11 vehicles damaged
Article Text:
A 52-year-old man was injured when a tree unexpectedly fell on him in Thane's Wagle Estate, also damaging eleven parked vehicles





THANE: A 52-year-old man sustained head and chest injuries after a tree suddenly collapsed on him near Wagle Police Station, Tuesday morning. The fallen tree also damaged 11 parked vehicles, including a police bomb squad bus.This is the first tree fall injury reported this season raising questions over the monsoon preparedness of the Corporation.The incident was reported around 9am near Prashant Corner on Road No 31 in Wagle Estate."The pedestrian, identified as Pawan Jha, 52, a resident of Sai Dham Society near Pipeline, Dnyaneshwar Nagar, was on his way to work when he was struck by the falling tree. He suffered injuries to his head and chest and was admitted to Pragati Hospital in Wagle Estate for treatment," informed Yasin Tadvi, incharge of RDMC, Thane.The damaged vehicles include a Maharashtra Police Bomb Squad bus attached to Wagle Police Station, a light commercial vehicle, two cars, one autorickshaw, and six two-wheelers.Personnel from Wagle Police Station, the Tree Authority Department, the Disaster Management Cell, and the Fire Brigade rushed to the spot with rescue, fire, and pickup vehicles. Authorities removed the debris and cleared the affected area.
Article Summary: A 52-year-old man sustained head and chest injuries after a tree suddenly collapsed on him near Wagle Police Station, Tuesday morning. The fallen tree also damaged 11 parked vehicles, including a police bomb squad bus. This is the first tree fall injury reported this season raising questions over the monsoon preparedness of the Corporation.

Article Title: Minor hits four vehicles with tempo, booked
Article Text:
Pune:

TOI





A 17-year-old boy hit four vehicles with a tempo he was driving to escape police interception on the Pune-Solapur highway in Vaiduwadi on Tuesday evening.The Wanowrie police registered a case of rash and negligent driving against the boy. The police also booked the vehicle owner for letting a minor drive.No one was injured in the incident. A traffic police constable managed to escape unhurt. The vehicle was stopped after the traffic police personnel from the Wanowrie traffic division chased it and intercepted it near the Number 15 signal in Hadapsar.The incident was recorded by CCTV cameras and dash cameras of some vehicles.Deputy commissioner of police (Traffic) Sandip Bhajibhakare told, “During a special enforcement drive against vehicles with tinted glass and fancy registration numbers, the traffic police attempted to stop a tempo after receiving a wireless message from the Wanawadi traffic division that the vehicle had ignored police signals and was being driven rashly.”“Our team tried to intercept the tempo at Vaiduwadi, but the driver failed to stop and continued driving rashly. The personnel chased the vehicle and finally intercepted it at Hadapsar. The tempo had already hit four vehicles before being intercepted,” he said, adding that the four damaged vehicles also left the spot.“Some people from the public beat up the tempo driver. Police personnel intervened and took him to the Hadapsar police station. The complaint was then forwarded to the Wanowrie police station for further probe,” he said.“The tempo is owned by a man from Kedgaon on the Pune-Solapur highway,” he said.
Article Summary: A 17-year-old boy hit four vehicles with a tempo he was driving to escape police interception on the Pune-Solapur highway in Vaiduwadi on Tuesday evening. The Wanowrie police registered a case of rash and negligent driving against the boy. The police also booked the vehicle owner for letting a minor drive.

Article Title: Restaurants in Pune caught between customer demands and World Cup screening rights
Article Text:
Pune:





As football fever grips the city, its restaurants, cafés, and sports bars find themselves caught in a high-stakes dilemma: meeting surging customer demand while navigating a growing legal “grey area” over commercial screening rights.While football-themed menus, prediction contests, and late-night screenings have begun appearing across the city, many establishments are now proceeding with extreme caution. Reports have emerged that several venues in Pune, Gujarat, and Bengaluru have received legal notices regarding unauthorised commercial screening of FIFA World Cup matches.“We are currently restricted from carrying out promotional activities centered around the matches,” said Saili Jahagirdhar, Pune chapter head of National Restaurant Association of India (NRAI). “A controversy is unfolding nationwide. The official rights holder has been sending notices to pubs and restaurants, demanding payment for commercial screening rights.”According to Jahagirdhar, the core of the dispute lies in the type of subscription used. “Many restaurant owners ask a simple question: if they are already paying their service provider, why should they pay again? The response they receive is that standard subscriptions are for domestic viewing only, not commercial use.The NRAI is currently evaluating the legal position and drafting an advisory. Until then, we are advising members to avoid monetising screenings through specific offers that could attract legal action. ”This uncertainty comes at a precarious time for an industry still finding its footing.“Restaurants have faced a string of challenges since the pandemic, with recent LPG price hikes adding to the strain,” said Ganesh Shetty, president, Pune Restaurant and Hoteliers Association (PRAHA). “Naturally, many businesses view the World Cup as a vital opportunity to bring customers back.”Shetty noted that FIFA-themed offerings have been a staple of Pune’s nightlife for decades, starting in hubs like Koregaon Park and Kalyaninagar before spreading city-wide. “Customers want the entertainment and the communal experience. If we don’t provide it, they simply go elsewhere,” he added, noting that PRAHA is also looking into the matter.To mitigate risk, some venues are pivoting to alternative formats. While some have suspended live screenings entirely until the legal fog clears, others are replaying matches during daylight hours or focusing on “football-inspired” menus and prediction games. Several malls have also stepped in, screening the 10:30 PM matches until closing time.For the fans, however, the draw remains the shared experience.“Watching a World Cup game alone at home is nothing like watching it with a crowd,” said Aditya Kulkarni, a 28-year-old software engineer. “The atmosphere, the collective reactions, and the banter with strangers are what make the tournament special. I’d even go for a replay just to be with friends.”Marketing professional Aaron D’Souza agreed: “The themed menus are a nice touch, but it’s the shared excitement that matters. During the World Cup, the whole city feels connected. You want to be out there with other fans.”For Pune’s hospitality sector, that enthusiasm is both an opportunity and a risk. While the crowds are ready to fill the seats, the question of who owns the “view” remains an unresolved hurdle for business owners.
Article Summary: Several venues in Pune, Gujarat, and Bengaluru have received legal notices regarding unauthorised commercial screening of FIFA World Cup matches. Some venues are pivoting to alternative formats. Others are replaying matches during daylight hours or focusing on “football-inspired” menus and prediction games.

Article Title: Nagpur paddlers disappoint, Mumbai dominate
Article Text:
The podium finishers of various categories with the guests on Tuesday





Nagpur: Paddlers from Mumbai Sub-Urban (TST) dominated the concluding day of the first Maharashtra State ranking table tennis tournament at the Subhedar Hall on Tuesday.While the Mumbai players won three of the six gold medals on offer, Solapur’s Rajvardhan Tiwari and Keshika Purkar of Nashik clinched the U-11 boys and U-13 girls’ titles, respectively. Players from hosts Nagpur, however, failed to win a single medal in the five-day tournament to end their campaign on a disappointing note.Later, the prizes were distributed by Yatin Tipnis, secretary general, MSTTA. On the occasion, Ashutosh Potnis, secretary, MSTTA, Shekhar Tongo, Prakash Jasani, Ashwin Ramteke, Akhilesh Potnis, Rajesh Mopkar, Prafful Nandedkar and Deepak Kanetkar were present.RESULTSU-11 Girls (Semifinals): 9-Dhadkkan Tanna (TST) bt Kiara Lele (THN) 11-9, 11-5, 11-6; 2-Riddhi Singh (THN) bt 3-Erina Ahmed (PUN) 7-11, 11-6, 11-8, 7-11, 11-9; Final: 9-Dhadkkan Tanna (TST) bt 2-Riddhi Singh (THN) 11-5, 11-9, 16-14;Boys (Semifinals): 1-Rajvardhan Tiwari (SLP) bt Naman Agrawal (PNA) 11-4, 11-5, 11-4; 2-Naksh Mahajan (PUN) bt 3-Adhiraj Chauhan (THN) 11-9, 15-13, 8-11, 11-9; Final: 1-Rajvardhan Tiwari (SLP) bt 2-Naksh Mahajan (PUN) 11-2, 11-6, 8-11, 11-5;U-13 Girls (Semifinals): 1-Keshika Purkar (NSK) bt 4-Vedika Jaiswal (TST) 11-4, 11-6, 11-5; 3-Jinaya Vadhan (THN) bt 7-Mahika Susvirkar (THN) 11-6, 11-5, 11-5; Final: 1-Keshika Purkar (NSK) bt 3-Jinaya Vadhan (THN) 5-11, 11-7, 11-7, 11-7;Boys (Semifinals): 5-Yashan Kolah (MCD) bt Nabh Pancholia (PUN) 9-11, 5-11, 11-3, 11-9, 11-8; Yuvaan Walia (MCD) bt 7-Arya Murti (THN) 11-3, 11-7, 11-5; Final: Yuvaan Walia (MCD) bt 5-Yashan Kolah (MCD) 11-5, 11-7, 11-5;U-15 Girls (Semifinals): 1-Aadya Baheti (PRB) bt Vedika Jaiswal (TST) 11-8, 9-11, 11-8, 11-5; Final: 2-Myraa Sangelkar (TST) bt 6-Dnyanashree Tarde (THN) 11-9, 13-11, 11-7; Final: 2-Myraa Sangelkar (TST) bt 1-Aadya Baheti (PRB) 11-7, 11-6, 11-5;Boys (Semifinals): 1-Param Bhiwandkar (TST) bt 5-Raghav Mahajan (TST) 11-8, 11-6, 11-9; 7-Ahan Gosar (TST) bt 3-Aarav Vora (TST) 11-8, 11-6, 11-6;Final: 1-Param Bhiwandkar (TST) bt 7-Ahan Gosar (TST) 11-6, 11-9, 4-11, 11-9.
Article Summary: Mumbai players won three of the six gold medals on offer. Solapur’s Rajvardhan Tiwari and Keshika Purkar of Nashik clinched the U-11 boys and U-13 girls’ titles, respectively. Players from hosts Nagpur, however, failed to win a single medal in the five-day tournament.

Article Title: Third seed tumbles on Day 1
Article Text:
GR Divya Deshmukh makes a move to inaugurate the U-7 National chess championship in Nagpur on Tuesday

Nagpur:





Rudranil Karmakar of West Bengal surprised Andhra Pradesh’s third seed Dharmik Panchakatla to cause the first major upset on the opening day of the U-7 National Chess Championships, which began at the GH Raisoni Institute of Engineering and Technology hall, Shraddha Park, Wadi-Hingna Link Road, on Tuesday.After losing the opening tie against unrated Rudranil, Dharmik was held to a draw by Tamil Nadu’s Umar Abdul Aziz in the second round, while all the other seeded players won their respective contests to garner full points.In the second round of the Open group, top seed Vislavath Lavik outclassed Aveer Shah, while girls’ seed No. 1 Udhriti Bhattacharya defeated Ishanvi Rajbongshi to share the lead.Earlier, the nine-round national championship being held in Nagpur after a gap of 19 years was inaugurated by Women’s World Cup champion and city’s third Grandmaster Divya Deshmukh. After making the opening move to inaugurate the championship, Divya shared her experience of playing age-category national tournaments. After the opening ceremony, Divya hogged the limelight as parents and young prodigies were seen taking selfies with the World Junior No. 1 in the girls section.On the occasion, Parineeta Fuke, Vivek Kapur, vice chancellor of GH Raisoni Skill Tech University, AK Verma, the chief administrative officer of All India Chess Federation, Girish Vyas, Debashish Barua, Niranjan Godbole and Bhushan Shriwas shared the dais.Results (Round 2)Open: Vislavath Lavik (2) bt Aveer Shah (1); Ayaansh Fuller (1) lost to Sathwik Bhat (2); Badana Duvanth (1) lost to Shivansh Basu (2); Aksh Shrivastava (2) bt Daksh Kathrani (1); Bejugam Gupta (1) lost to Rudra Pratap (2); Daniel Lakra (1) lost to Ivaan Dubey (2); Joshua Katarapu (2) bt Devaditya Jain (1); Akshath Chowhan (2) bt Devvishwar R (1); Pugazh Vinoth (1) lost to Dhruv Ponnoth (2); Vivaan Kadam (2) bt Divyansh Pruthi (1);Girls: Udhriti Bhattacharya (2) bt Ishanvi Rajbongshi (1); Tanishqa Jain (1) lost to Aarini Lahoty (2); Aaditri Majumdar (2) bt Jaahnavi Manohar (1); Mugdha Kelkar (1) lost to Shazwa Shaik (2) ; Navya R (2) bt Niyara Reddy (1); Pakhi Patel (1) lost to Aaradhya Kommery (2); Dia Baisnab (2) bt Parjanyaa Borah (1); Pranave Sri (1) lost to Liyaa Krishnan (2); Rayanika Shivaram (2) bt Prajakta Dhar (1); Prisha Shikeria (2) bt Aashvi VA (1).
Article Summary: Rudranil Karmakar of West Bengal surprised Andhra Pradesh’s third seed Dharmik Panchakatla. Top seed Vislavath Lavik outclassed Aveer Shah, while girls’ seed No. 1 Udhriti Bhattacharya defeated Ishanvi Rajbongshi to share the lead.

Article Title: Weekly special train services running via Coimbatore extended
Article Text:
Representative image





COIMBATORE: The service period of weekly special trains operated between Bengaluru-Thiruvananthapuram has been extended till July 30.According to a press release, No. 06547 SMVT Bengaluru-Thiruvananthapuram North weekly special train will leave SMVT Bengaluru at 7.25pm on July 1, 15, 22 and 29 Wednesdays) to reach Thiruvananthapuram North at 1.15pm the next day.No. 06548 Thiruvananthapuram North-SMVT Bengaluru weekly special train will leave Thiruvananthapuram North at 3.15pm on July 2, 16, 23 and 30 (Thursdays) to reach SMVT Bengaluru at 8.30 am the next day.These trains will have stoppages at Krishnarajapuram, Bangarapet, Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Podanur, Palakkad, Thrissur, Aluva, Ernakulam Town, Kottayam, Changanassery, Thiruvalla, Chengannur, Mavelikara, Kayamkulam, Sasthankotta, Kollam and Varkala.
Article Summary: The service period of weekly special trains operated between Bengaluru- Thiruvananthapuram has been extended till July 30. The trains will have stoppages at Krishnarajpuram, Bangarapet, Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Podanur, Palakkad, Thrissur, Aluva, Ernakulam Town.

Article Title: Three arrested with 4.3kg ganja in Coimbatore
Article Text:
Prohibition enforcement wing police in Coimbatore city on Tuesday arrested three men for smuggling ganja in a car near Sungam Junction

Coimbatore:





The prohibition enforcement wing (PEW) on Tuesday arrested three people for peddling ganja in a car near Sungam Junction in the city.The arrested were identified as P Manimaran, 28, of Podanur ; K Navarathinavel, 39, of Sundarapuram ; and D Harishankar, 28, of Sungam.They were apprehended after a team led by PEW inspector Miadit Mano conducted a vehicle check at Sungam Junction, following a tip off. Police intercepted a white car in which the three accused were travelling and found 4.3kg of ganja in the car.Police seized the contraband, along with the car and a two-wheeler used by the accused. All three were later produced before a court and remanded in judicial custody.
Article Summary: The prohibition enforcement wing (PEW) on Tuesday arrested three people for peddling ganja in a car near Sungam Junction in the city. The arrested were identified as P Manimaran, 28, of Podanur ; K Navarathinavel, 39, of Sundarapuram ; and D Harishankar, 28. All three were later produced before a court and remanded in judicial custody.

Article Title: NEET: Salem division to run special trains, announces helplines
Article Text:
Representative image





COIMBATORE: The Salem division of Southern Railway has made special arrangements to facilitate the candidates appearing for the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET-UG) on Sunday.There are seven towns in the jurisdiction of Salem division where NEET will be conducted. They are Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Coimbatore, Ooty , Namakkal and Karur. Special arrangements have been made across the division, including operation of special trains, to facilitate smooth movement of candidates.Two special trains – one from Chinna Salem to Salem Jn and the other from Tirupattur to Salem Jn – will be operated on Sunday to facilitate candidates appearing for the NEET.Traffic at important level crossing gates will be monitored and regulated to avoid road congestion and ensure smooth movement of candidates to examination centres.Helpdesks will be available at Salem Jn, Erode Jn, Tirupur, Coimbatore Jn, Udagamandalam, Namakkal and Karur Jn railway stations to provide travel related information and assistance to candidates.The helplines: Salem Jn 8778998561, Erode Jn 9840575966, Tiruppur 7904724532, Coimbatore Jn 9384863048, Ooty 7338946729, Namakkal9443516702 and Karur Jn 8056256965.
Article Summary: There are seven towns in the jurisdiction of Salem division where NEET will be conducted. They are Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Coimbatore, Ooty, Namakkal and Karur. Special arrangements have been made across the division.

Article Title: Boy drowns in floodwaters near Erode
Article Text:
Representative image





Erode: A six-year-old boy drowned after being swept away by floodwaters in a stream near Thalavadi in Erode district on Sunday evening. The body of Madhan Karthik, son of Rangaraj of Diginarai village, was recovered on Monday evening. He was a Class II student.Following heavy rain in the Sathyamangalam Tiger Reserve, the stream in Diginarai village was overflowing on Sunday evening.Karthik went to see the floodwaters after telling his parents, but he did not return home.His parents and relatives searched for him near the stream, but they did not find him. It later emerged that the boy was swept away by the floodwaters. They informed the Thalavadi police, who rushed to the spot and joined searches for the boy.Later, fire and rescue services personnel from Hasanur also reached the scene and joined the searches. They retrieved the boy's body from the stream close to Swarnavathi river in Chamarajanagar in Karnataka on Monday. The river is the tributary of the Cauvery. The body was sent to the Sathyamangalam government hospital for postmortem.A case was registered.
Article Summary: The body of Madhan Karthik, son of Rangaraj of Diginarai village, was recovered on Monday evening. He was a Class II student. Following heavy rain in the Sathyamangalam Tiger Reserve, the stream was overflowing on Sunday evening.
